metadata.title: A2 Key Gold Mock 2
metadata.description: M4.1 academic mock for A2 Key — travel, work, and daily life themes across reading, writing, listening, and speaking.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

parts.title: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture
parts.instructions: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

audio.transcript: Man: I think I've got everything — passport, tickets, and my suitcase is ready by the door.
audio.caption: Part 1 — Listen and choose the correct picture

parts.title: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer
parts.instructions: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

audio.transcript: Guest: We picked this hotel because it's only five minutes from the beach. Receptionist: Yes, many families choose it for that reason.
audio.caption: Part 2 — Listen and choose the best answer

parts.title: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap
parts.instructions: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Guide: Welcome to the walking tour. We start at ten o'clock. Please wait at the main reception desk. The next bus to the old town leaves in fifteen minutes.
audio.caption: Part 3 — Listen and complete each gap

audio.transcript: Speaker: I love travelling because I can try food I've never tasted before. I started my current job two years ago, but I still save money for trips abroad.
audio.caption: Part 4 — Listen to the monologue

parts.title: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity
parts.instructions: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

audio.transcript: Manager: Emma works the morning shift on Monday. Tuesday we run a coastal walking tour. Wednesday is kitchen cleaning day, Thursday is first aid training, and Friday we offer a weekend city break.
audio.caption: Part 5 — Listen and match each day to an activity

questions.questionText: PASSENGERS MUST SHOW TICKETS BEFORE BOARDING

choices.text: You need a ticket to get on the train.
Tickets are sold on the train.

questions.questionText: Job vacancy — Saturday shop assistant. Apply in store.

choices.text: The shop is looking for someone to work on Saturdays.
The shop is closed on Saturdays.
You must apply by email only.

questions.questionText: Community centre — Free laundry service every Tuesday.

choices.text: You can wash clothes at no cost on Tuesdays.
The laundry is open every day.
You must pay for the service.

questions.questionText: Flight 247 to Rome — boarding begins at Gate 8 at 14:15.

choices.text: Passengers should go to Gate 8 before 14:15.
Passengers can board from 14:15 at Gate 8.

questions.questionText: Office closed today — staff training at the conference centre.

choices.text: Workers are at a training event, not in the office.
Visitors can meet staff at the office.

questions.questionText: Bus replacement service — trains not running between Central and North until 18:00.

choices.text: Passengers must use buses on part of the route.

questions.questionText: Who prefers travelling by bicycle?
Who works in a hospital?

pairs.rightText: is a nurse on the children's ward

questions.questionText: Who is learning to cook traditional dishes?
Who visited three countries last summer?
Who delivers packages in the local area?

pairs.rightText: drives a van for a delivery company

questions.questionText: Who is going to move to a new flat next month?

pairs.rightText: has found a larger apartment near the park

questions.questionText: Who has started a weekend market stall?

pairs.rightText: sells handmade jewellery at the town market

questions.questionText: Where does Maria work?

content.passage: Last month, Maria started her first part-time job at a busy city hotel. She works at the reception desk three afternoons a week after school. On her first day, she learned how to check guests in and answer phone calls. The hotel manager, Mr Chen, showed her where to find room keys and how to use the computer system. Maria enjoys meeting travellers from different countries and helping them find local restaurants. She saves most of her wages for a summer trip to Portugal. Her colleagues say she is friendly and organised, and she hopes to work there again next year.

questions.questionText: How often does Maria work at the hotel?

choices.text: She wants to leave the job

questions.questionText: Gap (1): plan your journey in ___

content.passage: When you travel by train, it is useful to plan your journey in (1) ___. Check the departure time online and arrive at the station at least ten minutes (2) ___. Keep your ticket in your bag because an inspector may ask to see (3) ___. If you need to change trains, read the information board carefully and follow the signs to the correct (4) ___. Pack a light snack and a bottle of water for longer trips. Finally, remember to take your luggage with you when you (5) ___ the train at your destination. Many stations also have lockers if you want to store a bag for a few (6) ___.

questions.questionText: Write an email to your manager.

content.prompt: You cannot come to work on Friday because you need to travel to another city. Write an email to your manager.
content.taskDescription: Write 25–35 words. Include all three points.
requiredPoints: Explain why you cannot come to work on Friday
Say when you will return to work
Offer to finish an important task before you leave

questions.questionText: Write a story about a missed train.

content.prompt: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story. Your story must begin with this sentence: 'I arrived at the station just as the train was leaving.'

Why did the family choose the hotel?

What are they going to do this weekend?
How did Lisa get to the conference?
What does the supervisor tell the new worker?

choices.text: Always wear safety shoes in the warehouse

questions.questionText: What does the speaker enjoy most about travelling?
When did the speaker start her current job?
Why does the speaker walk to the shops?

choices.text: It is good exercise and saves money

questions.questionText: Which country does the speaker want to visit next?
What is the main reason the speaker changed careers?

choices.text: She wanted more contact with people

questions.questionText: Which shift does Emma work on Monday?
Which destination is offered on Tuesday?
Which household task is planned for Wednesday?
Which training session is on Thursday?
Which trip departs on Friday?
Answer the examiner's questions about work.

content.prompt: The examiner will ask you about your daily life, any jobs you have, and places you like to visit.
followUpQuestions: What do you usually do after school or work?
Have you ever had a part-time job?
Where would you like to travel in the future?

questions.questionText: Describe the travel picture and say what the people are doing.

content.prompt: Look at the photograph of a busy train station. Describe what you can see and say where the people might be going.
